hero.title: Takumi Takasuka
hero.description1: Frontend Engineer / Data Engineer / Data Scientist
hero.description2: 1st-year student, Graduate Program in Science and Technology Innovation, Nagaoka University of Technology
hero.description3: Member of the Machine Learning Theory Laboratory
hero.extra_info1: I belong to the information bureau "NUTMEG," the organizing committee for the university festival, working to DX the festival through web applications.
hero.extra_info2: Leveraging these experiences, I also participate in various hackathons and LT events hosted by other organizations.
hero.extra_info3: In the Machine Learning Theory Laboratory, I work with meteorological data to tackle the task of generating weather forecast comments.

data.title: AI Research Institute Official Website
data.description: This is the official website of the AI Research Institute operated by LinkX Japan Inc. To enhance corporate credibility, the design is simple yet refined.

data.title: NUTMEG-Seeds
data.description: A web app for sharing technical knowledge within the group "NUTMEG," of which I am a member. The theme color is orange, NUTMEG’s image color, and attention to detail is applied throughout the design.

data.title: Group-manager-2
data.description: A web application used for registering at the university festival of Nagaoka University of Technology. It excels in functionality such as PDF export and statistical data display, featuring a stylish black-based design.

data.title: Communication-AI
data.description: A web application that enables conversation with generative AI. Although the conversation feature is simple, the design and accessibility are carefully considered so that it can be used by a wide range of users.

data.title: Slack-message-finder
data.description: A web application that retrieves and saves Slack logs in real time, displaying them on a Slack-like page. Features include channel-wise up/down display and weekly activity tracking for specific users.

data.title: Mentor-management-app
data.description: A web application that supports a mentor system. It links mentors and mentees and allows mentors to review mentees’ learning records. It is a simple app managed by administrators.

data.title: Validation of Weather Map Image Recognition Performance by ChatGPT
data.authors: Yuki H. Takano, Shotaro Watanabe, Gendo Kumoi
data.abstract: Weather map recognition is a complex image recognition task that requires two cognitive processes: interpreting meteorological symbols and making future predictions. Recent advances in multimodal AI suggest that such complex tasks can be addressed. In this study, we use ChatGPT, a type of multimodal AI, to evaluate the recognition performance on weather map images and propose prompt engineering techniques to achieve more accurate recognition. Weather map recognition is also featured in the Common University Entrance Test. If weather maps can be recognized, automatic grading and question generation become possible, contributing to learning support. Furthermore, it is expected that weather commentary text can be automatically generated from weather maps. Therefore, weather map image recognition is an important challenge that requires not only understanding local time-series changes in meteorological data but also recognizing information about large-scale atmospheric patterns such as pressure distributions and making future predictions. In this study, we use ChatGPT4-Vision, a multimodal AI, to verify the recognition performance on weather map images. We investigate whether it can answer questions about weather maps from university entrance exams and generate weather commentary text from weather map images. By conducting multiple experiments with varying tasks and information in prompts, and evaluating the quality of generated explanations, we propose and verify improvements in reading performance via prompt engineering.

data.title: Verification of Weather Comment Generation from Weather Map Images Using ChatGPT
data.authors: Yuki H. Takano, Shotaro Watanabe, Gendo Kumoi
data.abstract: Weather forecasts (weather comments) are created by meteorologists interpreting visualized information and expressing it in natural language. Existing studies on the automatic generation of weather comments face two challenges: incorporating information about large-scale atmospheric fields and generating natural text. In this study, we use ChatGPT4-Vision, a multimodal AI, to automatically generate weather comments from surface weather map images. We conduct experiments that vary the tasks specified in prompts and the amount of information provided, and then verify the accuracy of the outputs to explore the applicability of multimodal AI to weather comment generation. The results show that structuring prompts and clarifying instructions, as well as instructing step-by-step execution, are effective. Furthermore, few-shot learning demonstrates that ChatGPT4-Vision can understand and generalize new concepts and patterns. We expect that these findings will contribute to realizing personalized weather forecasts for each individual.
